pages.raw_text:
The Inland Empire Historical So
ciety, composed of pioneers. met at
the high school last night to outline
plans for a memorial ON the old
Steptoe fighting ground near Ro'
salia. The occasion brought to-
gether army Of the old timers.
among them being J. T. Elliott. of
Walla Walla; M. E. Choate, of Ro-
salia, and James Hayes. of Waverly.


A committee, consisting of Prof.
Trimble, ]. E. Strong and Mrs. G.
l.. Hathaway, was appointed to ar
range details of erecting and UNI
yelling the monument proposed to
commemorate the historic battle
with the Indians.


The meeting following the bust
ness session reverted into an inter
esting "reminiscences recital of
pioneer days, in which most of the
important events of that time were
revived. Col. Wright's fights with
the Indians about the present site
of Spokane were the subject Of ami
mated discussion. The wholesale
hanging of Indians that gave Hang
man creek its name was recalled.
